frozen_components: base model weights (both teacher and student)
architecture.summary: Prompt distillation is a self-distillation method for knowledge injection into LLMs. A teacher model with access to new knowledge K in its prompt generates logit distributions over answer tokens for question-answer pairs. A student model (initialized identically to the teacher, with a trainable LoRA adapter) receives only the question and answer, and is trained via KL divergence to match the teacher's logit distributions. The teacher remains frozen; only the student's LoRA adapter is updated. The method requires no external retrieval, no larger teacher models, and no structured knowledge formats—only free-form documents from which question-answer pairs are synthetically generated at high temperature for diversity.

biblio.title: Efficient Knowledge Injection in LLMs via Self-Distillation

baseline.name: supervised fine-tuning with same 30 training questions

claims.statement: Prompt distillation significantly outperforms supervised fine-tuning on Squadshifts closed-book QA and can match retrieval-augmented generation performance
Prompt distillation combined with RAG achieves the highest overall performance across evaluated datasets
Prompt distillation is substantially more data-efficient than supervised fine-tuning; to match PD performance with 20 training questions, SFT requires an order of magnitude more training data
Prompt distillation's higher data efficiency compensates for its additional forward pass, making it more time-efficient than supervised fine-tuning

Self-distillation (where expert = teacher = student model) outperforms using larger models as expert or teacher, because style mismatches between larger and smaller models overshadow benefits of greater model capacity
Using higher sampling temperatures for training data generation and distillation is critical to prompt distillation performance; reducing either temperature degrades results
Prompt distillation requires neither larger teacher models nor structured knowledge formats, enabling efficient internalization of complex factual knowledge from free-form documents
Supervised fine-tuning does not reach the asymptotic performance of prompt distillation, even with substantially more training data
